title: viburnum stellulatum var. involucratum
intro: Viburnum stellulatum var. involucratum, also known as the Korean spice viburnum, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Viburnum stellulatum var. involucratum include its ability to support immune function, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant effects, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries, leaves, and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or topically to harness its medicinal properties.
